# ForgeSpec MCP

> The coordination backbone for multi-agent AI development. MCP Server for Spec-Driven Development (SDD) â€” provides typed contract validation, SQLite-backed task board management, and advisory file locking to coordinate work across multiple AI agents. Works with any MCP-compatible client: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, and more.

## Why ForgeSpec?

Multi-agent AI development creates coordination challenges: conflicting file edits, lost context between agents, unstructured workflows, no quality gates, and no way to resume failed tasks. ForgeSpec solves all of these with:

- **Zero infrastructure** â€” Embedded SQLite, no external services
- **Universal compatibility** â€” Any MCP client (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, OpenClaw)
- **Instant setup** â€” `npx -y forgespec-mcp`
- **9-phase SDD pipeline** â€” Enforced confidence thresholds prevent premature transitions
- **Full audit trail** â€” Every contract, task, and reservation is logged
- **Cross-platform** â€” Tested on Ubuntu, Windows, macOS with Node 18, 20, 22

## Quick Start

- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/forgespec-mcp): Install via `npx -y forgespec-mcp` or `npm install -g forgespec-mcp`
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/lleontor705/forgespec-mcp): Source code, issues, and contributions
- [Full documentation](https://github.com/lleontor705/forgespec-mcp/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Complete tool reference with parameters and schemas

## SDD Pipeline

9-phase lifecycle with confidence thresholds:

```
init(0.5) â†’ explore(0.5) â†’ propose(0.7) â†’ spec(0.8) â†’ design(0.7) â†’ tasks(0.8) â†’ apply(0.6) â†’ verify(0.9) â†’ archive(0.9)
```

## Tools (19 total)

### SDD Contract Tools (6)
- `sdd_validate`: Validate contract against phase schema with confidence check
- `sdd_save`: Validate and persist contract to SQLite
- `sdd_get`: Retrieve contract by ID
- `sdd_list`: List contracts (filter by project/phase)
- `sdd_history`: Phase transition history for a project
- `sdd_phases`: All phases with transitions and thresholds

### Task Board Tools (10)
- `tb_create_board`: Create task board for a project
- `tb_add_task`: Add task with priority, spec ref, acceptance criteria, dependencies
- `tb_status`: Board status with tasks grouped by status
- `tb_claim`: Claim task (validates dependencies, auto-moves to in_progress)
- `tb_update`: Update status (auto-unblocks dependents on completion)
- `tb_unblocked`: List tasks ready to work on
- `tb_get`: Full task details by ID
- `tb_delete_task`: Delete task (backlog/done only), cleans up dependencies
- `tb_add_notes`: Append timestamped notes
- `tb_list`: List all boards (filter by project)

### File Reservation Tools (3)
- `file_reserve`: Reserve files/globs with TTL (default 15 min)
- `file_check`: Check for conflicts on file patterns
- `file_release`: Release reservations (specific or all)

## Configuration

- **Claude Code**: `claude mcp add forgespec --transport stdio -- npx -y forgespec-mcp`
- **Codex CLI**: Add to `~/.codex/config.toml` under `[mcp_servers.forgespec]`
- **Gemini CLI**: Add to `settings.json` under `mcpServers.forgespec`
- **OpenClaw**: Add to `openclaw.json` under `mcp.servers.forgespec`

## Environment Variables

- `FORGESPEC_DIR` (default `~/.forgespec`): Database directory
- `FORGESPEC_DB` (default `~/.forgespec/forgespec.db`): Full database path

## Versioning

Uses conventional commits with automatic semver bumping:
- `fix:` â†’ patch | `feat:` â†’ minor | `feat!:` â†’ major
- Release: `npm run release && git push --follow-tags origin master`
